name: Atlantic Union Bank
description_short: Atlantic Union Bank is a regional bank offering personal and business checking, savings, lending, and wealth management services across multiple locations.
description_long:
Atlantic Union Bank operates as a full-service regional bank with physical locations, including the New York Avenue Solutions Center in Washington, DC. The bank serves both personal and commercial customers with a comprehensive suite of financial products and services. Their routing number is 051403164, indicating they are a federally-recognized financial institution.

The bank offers personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Free, Loyalty, Preferred, ThankU, and PRISM Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, and HSAs. For lending, they provide mortgages, home equity loans, auto loans, personal loans, and land loans. They also issue debit cards with design customization options and provide digital banking through online and mobile platforms, including Zelle integration and bill pay services.

Atlantic Union Bank distinguishes itself by offering tiered business solutions for different company sizes through Essential, Enterprise, and Elite Business Checking accounts. They provide specialized commercial banking for specific industries including healthcare, nonprofits, real estate & construction, government contractors, and veterinary practices. Their wealth management division serves both individual and institutional clients with investment, retirement planning, and estate planning services.

As a traditional brick-and-mortar bank, Atlantic Union provides the stability and FDIC insurance protection of an established financial institution. However, they are a regional bank rather than a national institution, limiting physical branch availability. Their product offerings are standard for regional banks and do not include specialized services like credit repair, debt relief, or alternative lending for those with credit challenges.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: FDIC-insured deposits through federally-recognized banking status
Multiple checking account options tailored to different customer needs (Free, Loyalty, Preferred, ThankU, PRISM)
Comprehensive lending products including mortgages, auto loans, home equity, and personal loans
Industry-specific commercial banking expertise (healthcare, nonprofits, real Estate, government contractors, veterinary practices)
Wealth management services for individual and institutional clients including investment and estate planning
Digital banking with Zelle, bill pay, mobile banking, and online account management
Business succession planning and treasury management services for commercial customers
cons: Regional bank with limited physical branch network compared to national banks
No specialized credit repair or credit building products for customers with poor credit history
No emergency or payday alternative lending products for short-term cash needs
Website does not prominently display current interest rates for deposits or loans
No information about minimum balance requirements or account fees on the main site
best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking industry-specific banking solutions
Individuals seeking comprehensive personal banking with multiple checking account options
High-net-worth individuals and business owners interested in wealth management services
Commercial clients needing specialized banking for healthcare, nonprofits, or real estate industries
diagnosis: Atlantic Union Bank is best for personal and small business customers seeking traditional full-service banking with regional branch access and specialized commercial solutions. The main caveat is that this is a standard regional bank without specialized products for credit challenges, debt relief, or emergency financing—customers with poor credit or immediate cash needs should look elsewhere.
services: Personal checking accounts (Free, Loyalty, Preferred, ThankU, PRISM)
Savings accounts, money market accounts, and CDs
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Online and mobile banking with bill pay and Zelle
Wealth management including investments, retirement planning, and estate planning
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 647 New York Ave NW, Washington, DC 20001
